body.title: [sprint] Attach reality-graded verdict_strength to corpus entries (the ground-truth join)
body.description:
**Problem:** io_corpus records carry the join key (ticket_id) but only a transport outcome (ok/timeout/error) — never a correctness verdict. So replay can prove fidelity (reproduce what the LLM said) but not correctness (produce a working output). Any starve-curve scored today would be self-consistent, not ground-truth-closed. This is the real first ticket of the grounding-spine re-scope; it replaces T-self-model-existence-checker as the program's start.

**Proposed shape:** A SEPARATE reader/enrichment module (NOT an edit to the hot dispatch path) that joins each corpus entry to the ticket store by ticket_id and stamps a verdict_strength on the gradient: llm_said_it < test_green < executed_live < consequence_bearing < akien_noticed. Reality-tested filter defines the two top code rungs precisely (advisor 2026-07-05): executed_live = code demonstrably ran since close (imported by the rack / present in logs) — this certifies LIVENESS ONLY (didn't crash), NOT correctness; consequence_bearing = ran in a path where wrongness WOULD HAVE SURFACED (a downstream error, a corrective commit touching the same lines, or noticed friction). Emit an enriched, greppable verdict-indexed view that replay and pre-registration consume.

**CP1 residue (keep explicit in the module docstring):** executed_live grounds 'didn't break in use', NOT 'was correct'; full correctness terminates only at test_green-with-teeth or akien_noticed.

**Affected files:** NEW unseen_university/devices/inference/corpus_verdict.py (reader/enrichment; reads io_corpus JSONL + ticket store); io_corpus.py docstring note pointing to the enrichment reader (docs-live-in-code). Does NOT edit device.py dispatch.

**Design rules:** flat-file only (no SQLite); OOP for the stateful reader; docs-live-in-code on io_corpus.py.

**Scope boundary:** verdict_strength attachment + reality-tested filter definition + enriched view only. Does NOT run replay, does NOT modify dispatch capture.

**Test plan:** red→green. Fixture corpus JSONL + fixture ticket store with known verdicts. Assert each entry gets the correct verdict_strength. KEY proof: a wrong-but-non-crashing (liveness-only) entry must score executed_live and NOT consequence_bearing — goes RED on a naive presence-in-logs filter, GREEN on the consequence-bearing filter. Assert llm_said_it entries are excluded from the reality-tested view.

**Observable via:** log.info('corpus_verdict: joined N entries, stamped by strength {llm_said_it:a, test_green:b, executed_live:c, consequence_bearing:d, akien_noticed:e}, excluded M unlinkable') at the end of the enrichment pass — points at the join if the reality-tested view comes back empty.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:** corpus_verdict.py emits a verdict-indexed view where each entry has a verdict_strength on the gradient llm_said_it<test_green<executed_live<consequence_bearing<akien_noticed; the wrong-but-non-crashing fixture scores executed_live (not consequence_bearing) in a red->green test; llm_said_it entries are excluded from the reality-tested view.
body.intention: I intend that every corpus entry carries a verdict a self-built system could not author, so the starve-curve is scored against reality not self-agreement.
